page: Amazon Web Services r7g.xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by the custom 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton3 processor, specifically the Neoverse-V1 model. It features 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs running at 2.6 GHz with no hyperthreading. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong memory subsystem performance, with a Passmark memory score in the top 25% and a read-only cached memory bandwidth of 385.82 GB/sec. Large-block uncached read and write bandwidths reach 97.0 GB/sec and 39.6 GB/sec respectively. CPU performance is generally moderate, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores falling in the middle 50%. However, the server exhibits weaker performance in specialized tasks, scoring in the bottom 25% for ray tracing, image processing, and extended instructions, and in the bottom 10% for CPU encryption and medium-model LLM text generation.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the r7g.xlarge offers a dense memory profile on an energy-efficient ARM architecture, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require high-performance local storage or specialized cryptographic acceleration. It is well-suited for workloads such as Redis in-memory caching, database operations, static web serving, and software compilation, but is less optimal for intensive cryptographic tasks or large language model inference.
description: The Amazon Web Services r7g.xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized server powered by a 4-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor running at 2.6 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, but includes no local storage and has a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmarks show strong memory performance, with a Passmark memory score in the top 25% and read-only cached memory bandwidth of 385.82 GB/sec. However, CPU-bound tasks like encryption and LLM inference show weaker relative performance. Qualitatively, the server offers resource-dense memory on an efficient ARM architecture, making it highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads such as Redis caching, database operations, and software compilation, while remaining less suited for heavy cryptographic or GPU-free machine learning tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7g.xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized server featuring 4 dedicated Graviton physical cores, 32.0 GB of RAM, and strong memory performance ideal for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7g.xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized server with 4 dedicated Graviton cores, 32.0 GB of RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline.
tagline: An ARM-based, memory-optimized virtual server delivering high memory density and strong bandwidth for caching and database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 4-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 ARM64 processor running at 2.6 GHz
Equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, offering a high 8.0 GB per core memory ratio
Strong memory performance, ranking in the top 25% on Passmark memory benchmarks
Features a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps with no local storage
Best suited for memory-bound workloads like Redis caching and database operations
